text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, in a quaint little town nestled among whispering forests, there lived an old goose named Gybeaux. Gybeaux had seen many things - the dawn chorus, the full moon hanging low in the sky, and even puppies chasing their tails. But what she cherished most were the autumn leaves.

Every year, right before sunset, Gybeaux would head out to the old oak tree at the edge of town. There she'd listen intently to the songs of the warblers. Her favorite? The cerulean warbler. It would always sing,

"Oh look at those falling gems!"

On one autumn afternoon, as Gybeaux listened attentively, something unusual happened. A cluster of these lovely little birds formed a circle in the air, chanting softly.

"Grybeaux, you've seen us gather these leaves for years. What is it that you seek from us?"

Gybeaux blinked in surprise. "Oh, you're casting spells? Just
